note: Task brief called this 'slide 21 oscillation waveform ~400ns'. Actual source slide in our archive is the O47 slide 08 (transient spike train). The O52-named slide_21 file is a different graphic (p-diode dynamic response, ramp-rate dep.) and is already digitised in research_plan/oracle_queries/O52_slide21_extract/openai_response.md.

period_us.note: Stable driven period; this is the driver triangular wave period, NOT a free-running relaxation oscillation.

I_peak_mA.note: Y-axis labelled to 6 mA but peaks reach ~4.8 mA (5.0 mA within uncertainty).

I_baseline_mA.note: Visually pinned to zero between spikes; <100 µA detectability.

spike_rise_time_ns_10_90.note: Measured on spike #1 (centred at t=0.27 us). 10%->90% of peak current.

spike_FWHM_ns_approx.note: Derived from rise+fall measurement window (~40 px / 460.5 px/us ~= 87 ns total). Conservative FWHM ~60 ns.

energy_per_spike_pJ_from_slide10.context: steady-state firing with 100 nA excitatory input, area=111 um^2

ambition_check.note: Rise time uncertainty 23% (just above 20% target due to ~12 px transition window vs. pixel jitter).

driver_conditions_assumed.V_G1_V_estimate: 0.6 (consistent with thickox 2T spiking config; see slide 17/18)
